label: 102_civil rights_ocr_devos_department education
description: The push for charter schools and school choice policies gained momentum under President Trump, while Democratic candidates debated the issue in primary campaigns. The growing divide among Democrats on this topic highlighted its significance in national politics.

earliest_articles.title: ESSA and the shifting landscape at the US Department of Education
earliest_articles.excerpt: The year 2015 was momentous for American education for two somewhat contradictory reasons—it marked the 50th anniversary of the passage of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act (ESEA) and the passage of the Every Student Succeeds Act (ESSA). ESEA established a major federal presence in K-12 schooling for the…

earliest_articles.title: ESSA could face an uphill battle whether Clinton or Trump wins
earliest_articles.excerpt: Senator Lamar Alexander hailed ESSA as a means to return power to the states, but neither Hillary Clinton nor Donald Trump seems quite convinced that the states should be left on their own. Although neither candidate has a worked out approach to education—unlike any of the last three presidents—ESSA may be in for…

newest_articles.title: FAQs: Checking in on the Department of Education
newest_articles.excerpt: This is part of the “Why we have and need a US Department of Education” series, which seeks to examine the role of the U.S. Department of Education at a time when the president of the United States has called for the Department’s demise. It considers what the Department does to shape education policy and practice in…

newest_articles.title: Tracking lawsuits challenging the Trump administration’s K-12 education agenda
newest_articles.excerpt: This tracker was last updated on February 3, 2026. This project tracks ongoing legal challenges to the Trump administration’s executive actions related to K-12 education. This includes legal challenges to executive orders and non-binding guidance documents that threaten to withhold federal funding from K-12 schools…

central_articles.title: Donald Trump, Betsy DeVos, and the changing politics of charter schools
central_articles.excerpt: An emerging narrative says we are entering a golden age for charter schools and school choice policies. President Donald Trump rarely mentioned education during the campaign but pledged funding to encourage school choice, calling it “the civil rights issue of our time.” He nominated an education secretary, Betsy…

central_articles.title: Do the current Democratic politics spell doom for charter schools?
central_articles.excerpt: In prior posts, I argued that education is likely to play a major role in the Democratic primaries. This is due to several factors, including: the growing importance of education as a national issue, the unions’ continued power, and the possibility that no Democrat will emerge with a majority of votes prior to the…

central_articles.title: Democrats’ views on charters diverge by race as 2020 elections loom
central_articles.excerpt: Charter schools have been featured prominently in the news lately, with new poll data showing a growing racial divide in Democrats’ support for charters and Sen.
